name: p3_warm_day_dual_thermostat_comfort_band
description: Issue #249 P3: On a warm day with a dual-setpoint thermostat, classification arms the comfort band [comfort_heat / comfort_cool] in heat_cool mode instead of issuing HVAC off.
notes: (a) Scenario effectiveness: if _apply_comfort_band is removed from apply_classification, the
comfort_band_armed assertion fails — the band is never armed and the thermostat is left
in its prior state without a refreshed setpoint, risking comfort failure on a warm afternoon.
(b) Docs↔production alignment: docs/08-COMPUTATION-REFERENCE.md must reflect that warm-day
classification now arms a comfort band (not HVAC off + supervisory guards). This scenario
is the regression anchor that forces any doc update if the band arm is removed.
(c) Occupant experience: if this scenario fails to catch a regression, the occupant may return
home to a house that was never actively cooled (band not armed), breaching comfort_cool.
Regression proof: delete or skip _apply_comfort_band call in apply_classification → comfort_band_armed
fails; change band ceiling from comfort_cool to setback_cool → expect_band ceiling check fails.
The thermostat must enter heat_cool mode (dual setpoint) — set_hvac_mode(heat_cool) fires.

events.note: Warm-day classification. P3: _apply_comfort_band arms [comfort_heat=70 / comfort_cool=76] in heat_cool.

assertions.reason: P3: warm-day classification applied. Comfort band armed immediately after — no pure HVAC-off.
P3 guard: _apply_comfort_band must fire. If missing, thermostat is left without a refreshed setpoint — comfort_cool ceiling undefended on a warm afternoon.

Band must be [comfort_heat=70 / comfort_cool=76]. Occupied+awake on any day type uses the full comfort band — no floor suppression. Wrong ceiling (e.g. setback_cool=82) would leave thermostat 6F too permissive and occupant overheated.

verdict.summary: P3 warm-day classification arms dual comfort band [70/76] — thermostat defends comfort_cool ceiling autonomously
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied; comfort_band_applied [floor=70, ceiling=76, active=ceiling, mode=dual]; set_hvac_mode(heat_cool) + set_temperature(low=70, high=76)
verdict.expected_behavior: Occupant returns home to a thermostat that has been defending 76F all day — no surprise heat buildup. Floor=70F (comfort_heat) is the full occupied+awake band — no floor suppression on warm days.
